title: Getting started
heading: Getting started
text:
import { Steps, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The project has three packages that run independently: the frontend at the repository root, the backend in `server/`, and the chat worker in `chat-worker/`. The dashboard runs on its own; the CI/CD pipelines panel shows an error state until the backend is up.

title: Getting started
heading: Frontend
text:
<Steps>

1. **Install dependencies** from the repository root.

 ```bash
 npm install
 ```

2. **Start the dev server.** The dashboard runs on [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173).

 ```bash
 npm run dev
 ```

3. **(Optional) point at a different API.** By default the frontend calls `http://localhost:3001`. Override at build time:

 ```bash
 VITE_API_URL="https://api.example.com" npm run build
 ```

</Steps>

### Frontend npm scripts

| Script | Command | Does |
|--------|---------|------|
| `npm run dev` | `vite` | Start the Vite dev server on port 5173 |
| `npm run build` | `tsc -b && vite build` | Type-check then produce a production bundle |
| `npm run preview` | `vite preview` | Serve the production build locally |
| `npm run lint` | `eslint .` | Run ESLint across all source files |
| `npm test` | `vitest run` | Run the Vitest suite once and exit |
| `npm run test:watch` | `vitest` | Run Vitest in interactive watch mode |
| `npm run typecheck` | `tsc -b` | Type-check without emitting output |

:::tip
`npm test` runs in jsdom with `globals: true` and the setup file `src/test/setup.ts` — no browser, no database, no network required.
:::

title: Getting started
heading: Backend
text:
Requires a running PostgreSQL instance.

<Steps>

1. **Move into the server package and install dependencies.**

 ```bash
 cd server
 npm install
 ```

2. **Point at your PostgreSQL database.**

 ```bash
 export DATABASE_URL="postgres://localhost:5432/snabbit_dash"
 ```

3. **Create tables and load seed data.**

 ```bash
 npm run db:setup
 ```

4. **Start the API.** It listens on [http://localhost:3001](http://localhost:3001).

 ```bash
 npm run dev
 ```

</Steps>

### Going live with the CI/CD integration

By default the CI/CD adapter returns deterministic mock data. To pull real GitHub Actions runs, set **both** variables before starting the server:

```bash
export GITHUB_TOKEN="<a token with repo + actions:read scope>"
export GITHUB_REPO="owner/repo"
npm run dev
```

When both are present the adapter switches from the mock to the live GitHub Actions provider. See [CI/CD integration](/sdlc-sample-worflow/backend/integrations/cicd/) for details.

title: Getting started
heading: Chat worker
text:
The "Ask the docs" chatbot is a Cloudflare Worker that calls the Anthropic API (Claude Haiku 4.5).

<Steps>

1. **Install dependencies.**

 ```bash
 cd chat-worker
 npm install
 ```

2. **Build the search index** from the current docs. Re-run whenever the docs change.

 ```bash
 npm run index
 ```

3. **Set the Anthropic API key** as a Wrangler secret.

 ```bash
 npx wrangler secret put ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
 ```

4. **Deploy** or run locally for development.

 <Tabs>
 <TabItem label="Deploy">
 ```bash
 npm run deploy
 ```
 </TabItem>
 <TabItem label="Local dev">
 ```bash
 npm run dev
 ```
 </TabItem>
 </Tabs>

5. **Paste the Worker URL** into `WORKER_URL` in `docs-site/src/components/AskAI.astro`, commit, and let the docs site rebuild.

</Steps>

title: Snabbit Agent Console
heading: Snabbit Agent Console
text:
import { Card, CardGrid, LinkCard } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The **Snabbit Agent Console** is an internal AI workflow console for Snabbit's ops team. It is a dense, dark, Linear-grade dashboard for running SDLC agents — PR review, deploys, RCAs, alert triage — backed by a REST API and a live CI/CD integration.

The UI is purpose-built for power users who need high information density, keyboard-first navigation, and instant feedback.

:::note
This documentation is maintained automatically. On a schedule, a documentation agent reviews the codebase and updates these pages.
:::

title: Snabbit Agent Console
heading: What's in the box
text: Three packages in one repository — they build, test, and run independently. Vite 8 + React 19 + TypeScript 6 + Tailwind CSS v4 single-page dashboard. Most panels render from static seed data; only the CI/CD pipelines panel makes a live network call. Express 5 + TypeScript REST API backed by PostgreSQL. Pluggable CI/CD provider (mock by default, GitHub Actions when credentials are set). Dependencies injected, so tests need no database and no network. Stateless Cloudflare Worker that keyword-searches a bundled docs-index.json, then calls the Anthropic API (Claude Haiku 4.5) to answer questions using only the retrieved excerpts.

title: Testing
heading: Testing
text: Both packages use **Vitest**. The frontend adds **React Testing Library** and `@testing-library/user-event`; the backend adds **supertest** for HTTP-level assertions. Neither suite requires a database, a running server, or network access.

title: Testing
heading: Frontend
text:
```bash
npm test # run once (from repo root)
npm run test:watch # watch mode
```

### Test environment configuration

Tests run in a `jsdom` environment with globals enabled. The relevant section of `vite.config.ts`:

```ts
test: {
 environment: 'jsdom',
 globals: true,
 setupFiles: './src/test/setup.ts',
 css: true,
}
```

- `environment: 'jsdom'` — gives each test a simulated browser DOM.
- `globals: true` — makes `describe`, `it`, `expect`, `vi`, etc. available without imports.
- `setupFiles: './src/test/setup.ts'` — runs the setup module before each test file.
- `css: true` — enables CSS processing so Tailwind class assertions work.

### Setup file: `src/test/setup.ts`

```ts
import '@testing-library/jest-dom'
import { afterEach } from 'vitest'

// Keep tests isolated — persisted UI state must not leak between cases.
afterEach(() => {
 localStorage.clear()
})
```

The setup file does two things:

1. **Imports `@testing-library/jest-dom`** — this augments Vitest's `expect` with DOM-specific matchers such as `toBeInTheDocument()`, `toHaveAttribute()`, and `toHaveTextContent()`.
2. **Calls `localStorage.clear()` after every test** — `usePersistentState` writes the selected category tab and sort order to `localStorage`. Clearing it in `afterEach` prevents state from leaking between test cases and ensures each test starts from a clean slate.

### Test files — 37 tests total

| File | Tests | Covers |
|------|-------|--------|
| `src/App.test.tsx` | 4 | Top-level app rendering

All 7 tests render the full 12-agent `AGENTS` array. `userEvent.setup()` from `@testing-library/user-event` is used for all user interactions (clicking tabs, typing in the search input) to simulate real browser event sequences rather than firing synthetic events directly.

| Test | Asserts |

| renders a card for every agent | All 12 agent names are present in the document |

ters by search query | Typing "deploy" shows Deploy Bot and hides PR Reviewer |

The persistence test (`remembers category across remounts`) exercises `usePersistentState` end-to-end: the value is written to `localStorage` on tab change and read back on mount. The `afterEach` `localStorage.clear()` in `setup.ts` ensures this state does not leak to subsequent tests.

title: Testing
heading: Backend
text:
```bash
cd server
npm test
```

### Test environment configuration

Tests run in a Node environment. `server/vitest.config.ts`:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'

export default defineConfig({
 test: {
 environment: 'node',
 include: ['src/**/*.test.ts'],
 },
})
```

- `environment: 'node'` — no DOM globals; the backend never needs them.
- `include: ['src/**/*.test.ts']` — explicit glob so the server config does not accidentally pick up frontend test files.

This config is kept separate from the root `vite.config.ts` so the server suite does not inherit the frontend's `jsdom` environment or `setupFiles`.

### How each test builds the app

Every test (or test file) calls a `testApp()` helper that constructs a fresh Express application with injected test doubles:

```ts
createApp({
 store: createMemoryStore(SEED_AGENTS, SEED_KPIS),
 cicd: createMockCicdProvider(),
})
```

This means every test runs against a clean in-memory store seeded with `SEED_AGENTS` and `SEED_KPIS`. No database connection is established; no HTTP calls leave the process.

|------|-------|--------|
| `src/__tests__/api.test.ts` | 6 | REST endpoints via supertest |
| `src/__tests__/cicd.test.ts` | 6 | `summarizePipelines`, provider selection, mock data |

### `api.test.ts` — 6 tests
